mission.title: Install and Configure DigitalOcean Kubernetes (DOKS)
mission.description: DigitalOcean Kubernetes (DOKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. It is ideal for users looking for a cost-effective and user-friendly solution for container orchestration without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed solutions and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the DigitalOcean CLI
steps.description:
The DigitalOcean CLI (doctl) is required to interact with the DigitalOcean API and manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -sL https://github.com/digitalocean/doctl/releases/download/v1.80.0/doctl-1.80.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/doctl

```

> This command downloads and installs the latest version of doctl.
> Make the doctl binary executable.

steps.title: Authenticate the CLI
steps.description:
You need to authenticate doctl with your DigitalOcean account to manage resources.

```bash

doctl auth init --access-token YOUR_DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN

```

> Replace YOUR_DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN with your actual token.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on DigitalOcean with the specified version and node count.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1

```

> This creates a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes in the NYC1 region.
commands: doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig file to interact with your new cluster.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file with the credentials for 'my-cluster'.
commands: doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in your cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Consider enabling autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to optimize resource usage.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster update my-cluster --enable-autoscale

doctl k8s cluster update my-cluster --enable-monitoring

```

> This enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> This enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

doctl k8s cluster delete my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation prompt.

uninstall.title: Remove DigitalOcean CLI
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the DigitalOcean CLI if it is no longer needed.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/doctl

```

> This command removes the doctl binary from your system.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workloads and configurations.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

doctl k8s cluster upgrade my-cluster --version v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources to a file.
> This command upgrades the cluster to version 1.31.
> Verify that all nodes are updated and ready.
commands: doctl k8s cluster upgrade my-cluster --version v1.31.0

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the authentication token is invalid or expired.

**Fix:**
Re-authenticate using 'doctl auth init --access-token YOUR_DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not fully provisioned or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and retry the command. Check the cluster status with 'doctl k8s cluster get my-cluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are querying the correct namespace or create resources as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your account has reached the resource limits set by DigitalOcean.

**Fix:**
Check your account limits and either delete unused resources or upgrade your plan.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features including new API versions and improved resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer supported.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all workloads are compatible with the new API versions before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: doctl k8s cluster create my-cluster --version v1.30.0 --count 3 --region nyc1
doctl k8s cluster kubeconfig save my-cluster

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and your DigitalOcean account is set up.
